description_long: This object is a small, brownish crustacean with a segmented body covered in short, stiff bristles. Its exoskeleton appears hard and slightly glossy, with a rounded cephalothorax and a tapered abdomen. The legs are jointed and slender, with some ending in pointed tips, and the claws are visible but not overly large. The surface is mottled with lighter and darker brown hues, and the appendages are arranged symmetrically along the body. Antennae are present and extend from the head region, while the eyes are positioned on the sides of the head, appearing sessile. The overall form is compact and slightly curled, typical of a crustacean found in urban food markets or discarded on city streets.
description: A small, brown, bristly crustacean with a hard, segmented body, visible claws, and slender jointed legs. Its surface is mottled and slightly glossy, with antennae and sessile eyes.
description_view_0: Side view showing the curled body, prominent claws, and bristly legs.
description_view_1: Dorsal (top) view highlighting the segmentation, bristles, and symmetrical leg arrangement.
description_view_2: Opposite side view with clear visibility of the claws, legs, and body curvature.
description_view_3: Ventral (underside) view showing the rounded cephalothorax and the arrangement of the legs and claws.
